headline: Jim Cramer Says Forget Tech: Buy FedEx, Honeywell Aerospace, and GE Aerospace Instead
summary: Jim Cramer is steering investors away from NASDAQ and toward industrial blue chips, but one of his top picks fell nearly eight dollars even after landing what he called a ridiculous, record-breaking order. Here is why he still thinks the setup is worth it.

headline: General Electric (GE) Could Be 11% Overvalued On Hybrid Electric Flight News
summary: General Electric (GE) is back in focus after GE Aerospace reported a first high altitude hybrid electric test flight with NASA, BETA Technologies, and Boeing, alongside fresh GEnx 1B engine orders from Philippine Airlines and AerCap. See our latest analysis for General Electric. These hybrid electric milestones and new GEnx 1B engine contracts come after a period where General Electric's share price has eased 4.6% over the past month but remains up 19% over 90 days, alongside a 1 year total...

headline: How to Use GE Vernova Earnings to Tell if the AI Trade Is Dead
summary: Demand for  GE Vernova’s  power generation equipment amid the AI data center building boom has sent shares to levels no one expected.  Now, how the stock reacts to earnings on Wednesday can help tell investors if the AI trade is alive and well, or if more pain is coming to many stocks, beyond shares of Vernova.  It’s an important report for Vernova, with shares above $1,000, up roughly 700% since the company was spun out of  GE Aerospace  in 2024.

headline: Are Chinese Planes Ready to Challenge Boeing, Airbus Duopoly and Their Suppliers in Commercial Aviation?
summary: Latest reports suggest that European regulators have wrapped up a months-long testing process for the China-made C919 passenger jet without finding any major problems in the hardware. The jet, which has already been in service domestically for over three years, may still need software tweaks based on collected data from these flight tests and other […]

headline: AerCap Announces Order for 15 Boeing 787 Dreamliners
summary: AerCap Holdings N.V. ("AerCap") (NYSE: AER) today announced that it has placed a direct order for 15 Boeing 787-9 Dreamliners, which will increase AerCap's fleet of 787 aircraft to approximately 140. The announcement was made at the 2026 Farnborough International Air Show. The aircraft are scheduled for delivery through 2033.
